label: Graph theory

node.label: Graph theory
node.definition: Nodes, edges, paths, spectra, and connectivity support relational learning, search, and structured knowledge.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Graph theory is modeled as depending on Discrete mathematics & logic and feeding downstream nodes such as Causal inference, Graphs, hypergraphs & relational data, MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing.
examples: atlas downstream link to Causal inference
atlas downstream link to Graphs, hypergraphs & relational data
atlas downstream link to MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing
limitations: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting. Its direct dependencies include Discrete mathematics & logic.

dependencies.rationale: Graph theory rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Discrete mathematics & logic.
